name: One Credit Card

rewards.displayCategory: Top 2 spend categories of the month (5X multiplier)

additionalBenefits: India's first metal credit card — lifetime free
5X rewards on top 2 spend categories each month (requires spending in at least 3 categories)
Reward points credited on the 10th of the following month
No rewards redemption fee; no minimum points threshold for in-app redemption
1% fuel surcharge waiver (capped at Rs 400 per month)
additionalDetails: 5X multiplier applies to your top 2 highest-spend categories in a given month. You must spend in at least 3 different categories to unlock the 5X benefit.
Card is issued by partner banks: Federal Bank, SBM Bank, South Indian Bank, BOB Financial (BOBCARD), Indian Bank, and CSB Bank. Network and specific MITC terms may vary by issuing bank.

salaried: Subject to issuing partner bank credit approval criteria
selfEmployed: Subject to issuing partner bank credit approval criteria

internalNotes: Card details manually reviewed and verified by user on 2026-06-14.
Network confirmed as Visa, Mastercard, and RuPay — all three variants are actively offered depending on issuing partner bank.
Fees confirmed as Lifetime Free (nil joining and annual fee).
Base reward rate: 1 OneReward Point per Rs 50 spent across all categories.
5X multiplier: Top 2 spend categories of the month earn 5X (5 points per Rs 100 = 0.5 Rs value). Requires spending in at least 3 different categories in that month.
5X reward points are credited to the account on the 10th of the following month.
Reward point exclusions confirmed from official T&C: fuel, rent, cash withdrawals, wallet loads, and money transfers.
Fuel surcharge waiver: 1% waiver at all fuel stations, capped at Rs 400 per month.
OneReward Points value: Rs 0.10 per point on statement credit redemption. Minimum 500 points required for statement credit. No redemption fee.
Issuing partner banks include: Federal Bank, SBM Bank, South Indian Bank, BOB Financial (BOBCARD), Indian Bank, and CSB Bank. Card-specific MITC terms vary by issuing bank.
The card image downloaded is the partner bank logos grid — not the physical card face. A proper card face image needs to be manually sourced.
